subject: Mathematics (NCERT - Ganita Prakash)
chapter: Connecting the Dots (Geometry - Lines and Angles)

activities_projects.title: The Transversal Equation Solver
activities_projects.objective: Use algebraic expressions to find unknown angles in parallel lines
steps: Draw two parallel lines intersected by a transversal
Label a pair of alternate interior angles as $(3x - 10)^\circ$ and $(2x + 15)^\circ$
Set up an equation based on the property of parallel lines to solve for $x$
Calculate the actual measure of all eight angles formed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates algebraic solving with geometric properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct value of x and all eight angles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Proving the Parallelism
activities_projects.objective: Determine if lines are parallel using the Converse Theorems
steps: Draw two lines that look parallel but are slightly tilted
Draw a transversal and measure a pair of co-interior angles
If the sum is $178^\circ$ instead of $180^\circ$, write a logical proof explaining why the lines will eventually meet
Identify which side they will meet on based on the angle sum
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Euclid’s parallel postulate and converse theorems
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical rigor in the proof of non-parallelism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Zig-Zag Angle Sum
activities_projects.objective: Solve for 'middle' angles using auxiliary parallel lines
steps: Draw two parallel lines with a 'V' shape or 'M' shape between them
Given the outer angles, draw a third auxiliary parallel line passing through the vertex of the 'V'
Use the alternate interior angle property to find the measure of the total middle angle
Explain the necessity of the construction line
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops the skill of using auxiliary lines in geometric proofs
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the multi-step calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Angle Bisector Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the relationship between bisectors of parallel line angles
steps: Draw two parallel lines and a transversal
Construct the angle bisectors of a pair of alternate interior angles
Measure the angle between these two bisector lines
Prove whether the bisectors are parallel to each other
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovers advanced properties of angle bisectors in transversals
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of construction and proof (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Angle Sum Property of a Polygon
activities_projects.objective: Derive the formula for total angles in any polygon
steps: Draw a pentagon and a hexagon
Divide them into the minimum number of triangles by drawing diagonals from a single vertex
Calculate the total angle sum ($180^\circ 	imes 	ext{number of triangles}$)
Generalize the formula $(n-2) 	imes 180^\circ$ for an $n$-sided polygon
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Generalizes the triangle angle sum property to higher polygons
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct derivation of the $(n-2)$ pattern (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Optical Illusion Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Use geometry to debunk visual errors
materials: Zöllner illusion or Poggendorff illusion image
steps: Take a printed optical illusion where straight lines appear tilted
Use a ruler and protractor to measure the angles of the 'transversals'
Prove mathematically that the lines are parallel despite what the eye sees
Report on how the surrounding angles 'trick' the brain
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses geometric measurement to verify reality over perception
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of measurements and quality of report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tri-Parallel Line Problems
activities_projects.objective: Solve angles when three lines are parallel
steps: Draw three parallel lines $l \parallel m \parallel n$
Draw two different transversals that intersect all three
Given only one angle, find the measure of all other 11 unique intersection points
List which properties were used (e.g., Corresponding, Vertically Opposite)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Manages complex systems of parallel lines and transversals
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of all calculated angles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Staircase Design Project
steps: Design a side-view of a staircase where each step (tread) is parallel
If the stringer (the diagonal support) acts as a transversal, calculate the angle of each step
Ensure that if the stringer is at $30^\circ$ to the floor, all steps are perfectly horizontal
Explain how co-interior angles ensure safety in the design
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies geometric principles to structural design
activities_projects.evaluation: Drafting precision and mathematical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Linear Pair and Bisector Proof
activities_projects.objective: Prove the angle between bisectors of a linear pair is $90^\circ$
steps: Draw a linear pair of angles $\angle AOC$ and $\angle BOC$
Draw their respective angle bisectors $OX$ and $OY$
Use algebra (Let $\angle AOC = 2a$ and $\angle BOC = 2b$) to prove that $\angle XOY = 90^\circ$
Write the proof in a formal 'Given, To Prove, Proof' format
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical consistency and algebraic accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Star Polygon Angle Sum
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the sum of angles at the tips of a star
steps: Draw a regular 5-pointed star
Identify the triangles and the central pentagon within the star
Use the exterior angle theorem of triangles to find the sum of the angles at the 5 tips
Prove why the sum is $180^\circ$ for a 5-pointed star
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Solves advanced angle sum problems in complex figures
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful proof of the $180^\circ$ sum (10 marks)
